title: End of Eva DVD aspect ratio
display_title: End of Eva DVD aspect ratio
wikitext:
[]: End of Eva DVD aspect ratio             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Wed Apr 21, 2010 7:33 am I picked up EoE for about 35 bucks online, which was in perfect condition, however it was the , I guess, "non-anamorphic" version that has the sides cut off. Can anyone PLEEEEEASE help me find a version on amazon/google etc. as low cost as you can of course, with the correct aspect ratio? I beg of Eva geeks I must have anamorphic End of Eva :B

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Wed Apr 21, 2010 8:03 am Did you get the Manga Entertainment version? Its shit. Also, its the only good American release. My best advice is to just download and burn a better copy of the film to DVD, replace the shit DVD, and convince yourself that you bought the anamorphic version, or try a foreign distributor.

[]:             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Wed Apr 21, 2010 8:15 am Thats what I am looking for, a foreign copy if anyone can help me locate one. Ill try the "online" copy and burn it. And just include it in the case, but Man I want an official copy :( guess thats really hard. I do have the Manga Entertainment version

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Apr 21, 2010 8:39 am Manga let the rights expire, hopefully someone else will license and release a decent copy based upon the Renewal video master. In the meantime, there's a really fantastic fansub out there of The End of Evangelion by Zhentarim DivX. Do a Google search for it. As always, we do not condone piracy here on Eva Geeks. Search for it yourself, do not post links. Here's a side by comparison of the domestic DVD and the Renewal fansub I'm referring to. http://www.evamonkey.com/randomimages/manga-eoe-vs-renewal-fansub.jpg Note, each of the images are presented in unaltered 720x480.

[]:             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Wed Apr 21, 2010 8:42 am Thanks much, Ill try to "search" for it on "those sites" that use programs that rhyme with "orrent" :) Ill look for his release. Much Obliged, I do hope they re release it. Sucks that you can't find a foreign site to buy the Japanese version :(

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Apr 21, 2010 12:18 pm ProfessorKaos64 wrote: Sucks that you can't find a foreign site to buy the Japanese version :(

[]:             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Wed Apr 21, 2010 12:22 pm Haha well thats what I need help with :) I dont mind the Japanese all over the box, I just want an anamorphic version I can buy and call my own, rather than the workaround of an .avi file off the net.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Apr 21, 2010 2:09 pm ran1 wrote: Also, its the only good American release.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Wed Apr 21, 2010 2:16 pm I saw it first on a friend's VHS (also a Mangu release, if I'm not mistaken) tape, so FWIW, it was an improvement, even though the improvement was from, say, elephant shit to my own shit.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Apr 21, 2010 3:27 pm Oh, I didn't realize it had a VHS release.

[]:             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Wed Apr 21, 2010 4:41 pm BrikHaus wrote: Only good? No, it's just the only. There is nothing good about it. You're better off importing a copy from somewhere else.

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Wed Apr 21, 2010 4:42 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Manga let the rights expire, hopefully someone else will license and release a decent copy based upon the Renewal video master. In the meantime, there's a really fantastic fansub out there of The End of Evangelion by Zhentarim DivX. Do a Google search for it. As always, we do not condone piracy here on Eva Geeks. Search for it yourself, do not post links. Here's a side by comparison of the domestic DVD and the Renewal fansub I'm referring to. http://www.evamonkey.com/randomimages/manga-eoe-vs-renewal-fansub.jpg Note, each of the images are presented in unaltered 720x480.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Apr 21, 2010 6:19 pm ProfessorKaos64 wrote: Thats what Im after, a site or two I can import it from.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Thu Apr 22, 2010 9:33 am I hate to bring bad news, but the Japanese DVD of EoE Renewal ARE NOT SUBTITLED NOR DUBBED . Unless you speak Japanese fluently, NOT RECOMMENDED (plus, these DVDs are Region 2 playable only, so you either need a Region 2 player or a Multi-region Player)

[]:             Quote Post by Heromaster111 » Thu Apr 22, 2010 9:59 am in other words it's just easier to pirate that shit yo

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Thu Apr 22, 2010 11:43 am Or you could stand outside the GAINAX Hedaquarters and demand an original 35mm film reel. I plan on doing that if/when I got to Japan for my study abroad.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Apr 22, 2010 12:25 pm ran1 wrote: Or you could stand outside the GAINAX Hedaquarters and demand an original 35mm film reel. I plan on doing that if/when I got to Japan for my study abroad.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Thu Apr 22, 2010 2:30 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: So, the original aspect ration for the theatrical release WAS 4:3? NOT 16:9?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 22, 2010 2:37 pm Since people are posting sites to buy Death/EoE, here's one more decent place to buy it from: Regular Edition: http://www.cdjapan.co.jp/detailview.html?KEY=KIBA-1009 DTS Collector's Edition: http://www.cdjapan.co.jp/detailview.html?KEY=KIBA-1209 (But if you'd rather just get a fansub, use . At one of the Fanime conventions, Hiroyuki Yamaga said that Gainax thinks fansubs are fine as long as people buy the actual film once they're locally available. I guess Kara has a different stance on this... )

[]:             Quote Post by Heromaster111 » Thu Apr 22, 2010 4:57 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I guess Kara has a different stance on this... )
